rag_rules: Place output range guarantees in component type scopes using visible feature names and concrete bounds.
Use only visible AADL features or locally declared helpers in AGREE contracts.
Ensure type consistency in AGREE expressions and avoid package-qualified identifiers not in scope.
generic_rules: AGREE contracts must be placed in valid annex scopes matching component type or implementation.
Generated AGREE expressions should use only available types and identifiers from the model.
Avoid referencing AADL properties as AGREE variables unless explicitly exposed as ports or constants.
Validate that all identifiers in AGREE clauses are resolvable within the same annex or visible scope.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks.
Guarantees and assumptions must use valid AGREE syntax with proper type declarations.
Constants and equations in AGREE should be declared with correct scalar types: int, real, bool.
anti_patterns: Do not reference AADL package identifiers or property names that are not visible in AGREE scope.
Do not place implementation-level bindings in component type annexes.
Do not assume that AADL property values are automatically available as AGREE variables.
irrelevant_topics: type scope range assumption and guarantee
AADL Property Names Are Not AGREE Variables chunk 1
AADL Property Names Are Not AGREE Variables chunk 2
